generated_oml:
// C2: Physical Acting Components
instance HorizontalMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller managing torque, speed, and position feedback for horizontal motion of the gantry crane's hoist via stepper/servo motors"
]

instance VerticalMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller managing torque, speed, and position feedback for vertical motion of the gantry crane's hoist via stepper/servo motors"
]

instance ElectromagnetActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromagnetic device at the hoist's end for gripping and lifting container-representing pucks"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision encoder measuring angular displacement of the hoist's swinging motion, feeding real-time data to the digital twin's kinematic model"
]

instance Anemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring wind speed to account for environmental influences on crane stability (secondary to motion sensing)"
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance SwingAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance WindSpeedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Anemometer
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-derived kinematic model defining the gantry crane's motion dynamics, integrated into Trajectory Generator Service for path planning"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo Fusion360CADModel
]

instance SimulationServiceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation Service modeling the crane's operational behavior under varying loads and environmental conditions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KinematicModel
]

instance OperationalStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously logged crane operational state (position, velocity, torque) for historical analysis via TimescaleDB"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SwingAngleData
    DTDFVocab:fromData WindSpeedData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDB
]

instance TrajectoryComparisonData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data comparing executed trajectories (physical) with simulated trajectories (digital twin) for validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SwingAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance Fusion360CADModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Autodesk Fusion360 CAD tool used for modeling crane geometry and kinematics"
    DTDFVocab:enables KinematicModel
]

instance TimescaleDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing logged crane state data (timestamps, sensor readings) for historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalStateData
]

instance EclipseMosquittoMQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker (Eclipse Mosquitto) facilitating real-time data streaming between physical crane and digital twin services"
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance PythonGantryLib : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom Python module handling core twinning logic for trajectory generation, logging, and validation services"
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance DockerContainerization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular containerization (Python C4 containers) for deploying and isolating digital twin services"
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

// C6: Services
instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time service computing optimal paths for gantry crane puck movements using kinematic and controller models"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service logging continuous operational data (position, velocity, torque) from physical crane to digital twin for retrospective analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataTrendInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service comparing executed trajectories (physical) with simulated trajectories (digital twin) via MQTT broker for real-time synchronization validation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationThresholdAlert
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/2D visualization service showing physical crane state (motion trajectories, structural deformation) in real-time or historical contexts"
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionTrajectoryVisualization
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralDeformationVisualization
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection service identifying deviations between physical and digital twins for proactive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionAlert
]

instance SystemEvolutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing DarTwin notation for structured documentation of system changes (kinematic/model/controller updates)"
    DTDFVocab:provides ChangeDocumentationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight from Trajectory Generator Service providing optimal path planning for crane operations"
]

instance HistoricalDataTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical analysis insight from logged operational data enabling performance benchmarking and fault diagnosis"
]

instance ValidationThresholdAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dashboard alert triggered when distance metrics between physical and digital twin trajectories exceed predefined thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance MotionTrajectoryVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization of physical crane motion trajectories for real-time or historical analysis"
]

instance StructuralDeformationVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of structural deformation in the gantry crane system"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alert generated by Fault Detection Service when physical-digital twin discrepancies exceed confidence bounds"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ChangeDocumentationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured documentation of system evolution changes using DarTwin notation system"
]

// C1: Environment
instance LaboratoryEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryCraneSystem
]

instance GantryCraneSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Lab-scale gantry crane system designed for research and educational purposes with open-source, modular digital twin implementation"
    base:contains HorizontalMotorController
    base:contains VerticalMotorController
    base:contains ElectromagnetActuator
    base:contains SwingAngleEncoder
    base:contains Anemometer
    base:contains KinematicModel
    base:contains SimulationServiceModel
    base:contains OperationalStateData
    base:contains TrajectoryGeneratorService
    base:contains HistoricalDataLoggingService
    base:contains ContinuousValidationService
    base:contains DataVisualizationService
    base:contains FaultDetectionService
    base:contains SystemEvolutionService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The control flow from the digital twin to the physical gantry crane is mediated through the Control Application and Physical Crane Interface:
1. **Trajectory Generation and Execution**:
   - The Trajectory Generator Service creates optimal movement trajectories (e.g., with no residual swinging motion) and sends them to the Control Application.
   - The Control Application uses the Physical Crane Interface to execute these trajectories on the physical crane.
   - No explicit command message formats, validation mechanisms, or authentication protocols are mentioned in the document.

2. **Feedback and Validation**:
   - The Continuous Validation Service continuously compares physical measurements of executed trajectories with simulated trajectories, but this does not imply real-time adjustments or explicit feedback loops to the physical system.
   - Operators are notified via the dashboard if thresholds are breached, but no mechanisms for automatic conflict resolution or real-time command adjustments are described.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "GreenhouseDT employs a real-time synchronization framework leveraging an **InfluxDB database** for time-series data storage and an **ActiveMQ message broker** for event-driven communication between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin. While specific synchronization frequencies are not detailed, the system relies on pre-recorded virtual machine data, implying a batch-oriented or periodic update approach rather than real-time adaptation. The Air Quality Management exemplar uses **Microsoft Azure tools**, though exact time-scale specifics (e.g., latency or update intervals) are not provided; its focus is on **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** via AutomationML, suggesting a hybrid real-time/simulated workflow. The lab-scale gantry crane system operates via a **Python program connecting to Arduino controllers**, with no explicit mention of synchronization granularity or latency requirements beyond its deployment on a personal computer for real-time operation. No documented evidence of sub-millisecond or high-frequency control loops exists in the provided context."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** system employs a **self-adaptive digital twin constellation** where the physical greenhouse and its digital twin dynamically adjust behaviors in real-time, using **InfluxDB** for data storage and **ActiveMQ** for event-driven communication. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar leverages an **AutomationML-based digital twin** integrated with **Microsoft Azure tools**, enabling **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** via a **model-driven engineering pipeline**. The **gantry crane system** (C4 Model) is described as a **cyber-physical system with orchestration patterns** for real-time control, but no explicit mention of a **digital twin constellation** is provided. The **discussion** highlights that the exemplars use **containerization/virtualization** (e.g., VMs, Raspberry-Pi + sensors) for modular deployment but lack a **commercial digital twin framework**, instead relying on **ad-hoc orchestration** (e.g., ActiveMQ, AutomationML). The **resource management** in GreenhouseDT is handled via **pre-recorded VM images**, while Azure in Air Quality Management abstracts this into cloud-managed services. **No explicit architecture patterns** (e.g., microservices, event sourcing) are detailed, but the **Air Quality Management** exemplar’s **Azure pipeline** suggests a **cloud-native coordination layer** for interoperability."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** Digital Twin system operates within an **ad-hoc lifecycle management approach**, lacking formalized lifecycle governance. The system does not explicitly define structured phases like 'Design,' 'Development,' 'Deployment,' or 'Maintenance,' nor does it employ lifecycle notations such as DarTwin. Instead, it relies on **continuous operational adaptation** through real-time sensor data integration and iterative adjustments to the digital twin model. The lifecycle governance is implicitly handled via the **co-evolution of the physical gantry crane and its digital twin**, where changes to the physical system (e.g., sensor placements, control logic) are mirrored in the digital twin through manual or automated updates. No explicit transition management protocols (e.g., validation gates, migration scripts) are documented for phase transitions."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
- **Continuous Validation Service**: Compares simulated trajectories (generated by the kinematics model) with actual sensor measurements (e.g., encoder readings) in real-time. Discrepancies exceeding predefined **distance metrics** trigger **operator notifications** via the dashboard, rather than automatic adjustments.
- **Uncertainty quantification**: Implicitly addressed by confidence bounds in trajectory comparisons, though explicit methods for error quantification are not detailed.
- **Quality assurance**: Traceability is maintained through logged data (TimescaleDB) and the digital twin’s ability to reconstruct past states. No explicit mention of formal validation/verification protocols (e.g., ISO standards), but the system’s ad-hoc approach relies on iterative operator feedback.

The documents do not specify thresholds for automatic adjustments, but the validation service’s role is to **alert operators** to deviations, ensuring human oversight in maintaining system integrity.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **MQTT broker** (likely **Mosquitto**) facilitates inter-service communication between the digital twin components (e.g., Trajectory Generator, Simulation Service, Continuous Validation Service) via **MQTT publish/subscribe** messaging. The network topology is **centralized and local**, as all software components (including the broker) are deployed on a **single personal computer** (as implied by the deployment diagram). No explicit communication protocols (e.g., TLS/SSL) or bandwidth/latency requirements are mentioned, but the system operates in a **low-latency environment** for real-time validation. Reliability is ensured by the **Continuous Validation Service’s threshold-based notifications**, though no redundancy mechanisms (e.g., failover clusters) are described. Security protocols are not detailed beyond the implicit assumption of secure local network operation."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin system** is deployed on a **single personal computer (edge deployment)** with no cloud or on-premise hosting mentioned. The **TimescaleDB** database and all other services (Trajectory Generator Service, Simulation Service, Continuous Validation Service, MQTT Broker) are co-located on this device. Computational requirements are not specified, but the system relies on **Python-based services** and a **local database** for real-time processing. Scalability is not addressed, but the architecture is designed for **low-resource, single-node operation**. Deployment models supported include **monolithic deployment** (all services running concurrently on the same machine). Availability/reliability is ensured through the **Continuous Validation Service’s threshold-based alerts** and the implicit assumption of a stable, single-node infrastructure."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** system integrates internally via **InfluxDB** and **ActiveMQ**, enabling real-time data exchange between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar integrates with **Microsoft Azure tools** (e.g., cloud-based services for visualization and prediction) but does not specify Azure IoT Hub; its AutomationML-based twin relies on proprietary Azure pipelines for interoperability. The **lab-scale gantry crane** operates as a standalone system with no documented external dependencies or integration protocols, functioning solely as a personal-computer-based digital twin. None of the exemplars demonstrate enterprise-level interoperability or standardized protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) for horizontal integration with other Digital Twins or systems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Lab Scale Gantry Crane Digital Twin system **does not adopt any commercial digital twin frameworks** (e.g., Eclipse Ditto, Eclipse BaSyx). Instead, it follows an **ad-hoc approach**, using the following technologies and protocols:
  - **Database**: TimescaleDB for time-series data storage.
  - **Messaging**: Eclipse Mosquitto (MQTT) for real-time sensor data exchange.
  - **Containerization**: Docker for deployment and isolation.
  - **Visualization**: Grafana for real-time dashboards.
  No industry standards (e.g., AutomationML, IEC 61499) or compliance frameworks are referenced. The system’s interoperability is limited to proprietary protocols and Docker-based workflows."
]
